{"key_event":"TFG, ICU rise, Ethiopian intervention, al-Shabaab insurgency","reasoning":"Compared with the 1990s, formal state power improved slightly from near-total collapse because transitional institutions, AMISOM support, and some regional polities provided limited administrative and coercive capacity, but the TFG still lacked a durable monopoly of force and lost much territory to insurgents and armed networks. Society power rose modestly as Somaliland/Puntland institutions, remittances, telecoms, clan bargaining, Islamic courts, diaspora and business networks organized real authority, though war, displacement, piracy, and jihadist coercion kept autonomous civil society weak-to-moderate overall.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":4.3,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":1.8}
